data.reasoningText:
Let me analyze the issues in this MSBuild project:

1. **Validation runs too many times (cross-join/cartesian product)**: In `ValidateGeneratedCode` target (line 51-55), the `Message` task uses `%(ProtoFile.Filename)` and `%(ValidationRule.Filename)` — two different item groups batched in the same task. MSBuild does a cross-join (cartesian product) when you batch over two different item groups in the same task. 3 protos × 2 rules = 6 executions. The user wants sequential validation: each proto file validated with all its rules. To fix this, we need to batch on one item group in the target and iterate the other inside, or use nested batching properly.
   
   Looking at the user's actual complaint more carefully — they want each proto file validated against all rules sequentially, so 3 executions total (one per proto, each checking both rules), not 6. The solution is to batch only on `%(ProtoFile.Filename)` at the target level and list all rules inside.

2. **CS2002 duplicate source files**: The static `<Compile Include="**\*.g.cs">` pattern will pick up any `.g.cs` files, including ones already in the intermediate output path, causing duplicate source file errors.

3. **dotnet clean removes too many files**: The `CleanGeneratedCode` target removes `**\*.g.cs` from Compile but doesn't actually delete the generated files themselves. The `<FileWrites>` item group uses `%(Filename)` batching on the proto files, which should work as a transform, but I need to think through whether this is actually correct.

The transform expression with `%(Filename)` inside the path should produce the right entries, but the real issue is that `FileWrites` is a static item group outside any target, so `$(IntermediateOutputPath)` might not be set at evaluation time. And even if it is, the `CleanGeneratedCode` target only removes items from the Compile group without actually deleting the physical files.

The broader problem is that the `<Compile Remove="**\*.g.cs" />` glob is too aggressive — it would strip ANY `.g.cs` file from compilation, including hand-written ones like `Constants.g.cs` in the root. That's why `dotnet clean` is removing too many files. Plus, `FileWrites` in a static ItemGroup won't be properly tracked by MSBuild's incremental clean infrastructure, which expects these items to be populated inside targets and written to the cache data files.

   <!-- Validation rules for generated code -->
   <ItemGroup>
     <ValidationRule Include="rules\required-fields.rule" />
     <ValidationRule Include="rules\naming-conventions.rule" />
   </ItemGroup>
 
   <!--
     BUG 2 FIX: The original Message task batched over %(ProtoFile.Filename) AND
     %(ValidationRule.Filename) — two unrelated item groups in the same task.
     MSBuild performs a cartesian product (cross-join) in this case, yielding
     3 protos × 2 rules = 6 executions instead of the intended 3.
 
     FIX: Batch the target on %(ProtoFile.Filename) so it runs once per proto
     file, and reference @(ValidationRule) as a flat list inside each batch.
   -->
   <Target Name="ValidateGeneratedCode" AfterTargets="GenerateProtoCode"
       Inputs="@(ProtoFile)" Outputs="%(ProtoFile.Filename)">
     <!-- Validate each generated file against all rules -->
     <Message Text="Validating %(ProtoFile.Filename) with rules: @(ValidationRule->'%(Filename)', ', ')"
              Importance="high" />
   </Target>
 
   <!--
     BUG 4 FIX: The original Remove="**\*.g.cs" glob was too broad — it would
     strip ANY *.g.cs file from Compile, including hand-written ones like
     Constants.g.cs. Scope the removal to only the intermediate-output files
     that GenerateProtoCode creates.
   -->
   <Target Name="CleanGeneratedCode" BeforeTargets="Clean">
     <ItemGroup>
       <Compile Remove="@(ProtoFile->'$(IntermediateOutputPath)%(Filename).g.cs')" />
     </ItemGroup>
   </Target>
 </Project>

data.content:
Here's a summary of the **4 bugs** found and fixed:

### Bug 1 — CS2002 duplicate source warnings
The static `<Compile Include="**\*.g.cs">` glob matched the same generated files that `GenerateProtoCode` also added to `Compile`, causing duplicates. **Fix**: Removed the static glob; metadata (`AutoGen`, `DependentUpon`) is now set inside the target.

### Bug 2 — Validation runs 6× instead of 3×
The `Message` task in `ValidateGeneratedCode` batched on `%(ProtoFile.Filename)` **and** `%(ValidationRule.Filename)` simultaneously — two unrelated item groups. MSBuild cross-joins them (3×2=6). **Fix**: Batch the target on `%(ProtoFile.Filename)` and reference `@(ValidationRule)` as a flat transform inside.

### Bug 3 — Generated files not tracked across builds
`<FileWrites>` was in a static `ItemGroup` outside any target. MSBuild only persists `FileWrites` to the incremental-clean cache when populated **inside** a target. **Fix**: Moved `FileWrites` into the `GenerateProtoCode` target.

### Bug 4 — `dotnet clean` removes too many files
`<Compile Remove="**\*.g.cs" />` would strip **all** `*.g.cs` files, including hand-written ones like `Constants.g.cs`. **Fix**: Scoped the removal to only the proto-generated files via `@(ProtoFile->'$(IntermediateOutputPath)%(Filename).g.cs')`.
